function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
Under common-control recast accounting, identify two specific pre-merger revenue or cost flows among SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings that are eliminated in consolidation. For each, explain whether elimination inflates or deflates reported seller-segment economics.

arguments.prompt: In the SpaceX S-1 filing {{spacex_s1}}, identify any pre-merger intercompany revenue or cost flows among SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings that are eliminated in consolidation under common-control accounting. Look for specific examples of intercompany transactions, related party transactions, or eliminations between these entities. results.content:
# Intercompany Revenue and Cost Flows Among SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings Eliminated in Consolidation

## General Elimination Statement

The filing provides a blanket statement that **all historical related party transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X have been eliminated in consolidation** as a consequence of accounting for the Mergers as reorganizations of entities under common control. Specifically:

> "No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and **all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation**."

This statement appears in multiple places throughout the filing and is reinforced by Note 2, which states: "All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation."

However, the provided text chunks **do not separately quantify** the specific dollar amounts of intercompany revenue or cost flows that occurred directly among SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings prior to the mergers. The elimination is described only at a general level.

---

## What Can Be Identified or Inferred

### 1. xAI Recognized Revenue — Potential Intercompany Component

Under the bullet for "xAI commercial, licensing and support agreements," the filing discloses:

| Period | xAI Goods/Services Obtained (from Tesla) | xAI Revenue Recognized |
|---|---|---|
| 2024 | $191 million | Not disclosed |
| 2025 | $506 million | $2 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $34 million | $0.4 million |

The $2 million in revenue recognized by xAI in 2025 and $0.4 million in early 2026 is described in the context of xAI's agreements **with Tesla**. However, the filing does not break down whether any portion of this revenue originated from SpaceX or X as customers of xAI's AI services. If any did, those amounts would have been eliminated in consolidation after the respective merger dates.

### 2. X Holdings Advertising Platform — No Identified Intercompany Advertising

The filing discloses that **Tesla** purchased advertising on the X platform ($0.5M in 2024, $4M in 2025, $0 in early 2026). There is no disclosure of SpaceX or xAI purchasing advertising on X, nor of X purchasing services from SpaceX or xAI, in the provided text.

### 3. Aircraft Usage — No Identified Intercompany Usage

SpaceX invoiced **Tesla** for aircraft usage ($1M in 2023, $1M in 2024, $2M in 2025). There is no mention of xAI or X personnel using SpaceX aircraft, so no intercompany aircraft cost allocation among the three consolidated entities is identifiable from the provided text.

### 4. Boring Company Lease — Outside the Consolidated Group

X entered into an office space lease with a subsidiary of The Boring Company. Since The Boring Company is **not** part of the SpaceX/xAI/X consolidated group, this transaction would **not** be eliminated in consolidation and remains a related party transaction.

---

## Summary of Quantified Related Party Transactions (Not Eliminated — Tesla and Boring Company Are Outside the Consolidated Group)

| Transaction Type | Provider → Recipient | 2023 | 2024 | 2025 | Jan–Feb 2026 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Goods/services obtained by SpaceX | Tesla → SpaceX | $11M | $4M | $144M | $0.2M |
| Goods/services obtained by xAI | Tesla → xAI | — | $191M | $506M | $34M |
| Revenue recognized by xAI | xAI → Tesla | — | — | $2M | $0.4M |
| Advertising purchases | Tesla → X | — | $0.5M | $4M | $0 |
| Aircraft usage charges | SpaceX → Tesla | $1M | $1M | $2M | $0 |

These transactions are with **Tesla** (and The Boring Company), which are related parties due to Elon Musk's involvement but are **not** part of the consolidated reporting entity. Therefore, these amounts are **not eliminated** in consolidation and remain as related party transactions in the combined financial statements.

---

## Conclusion

The provided text chunks **do not separately quantify** specific pre-merger intercompany revenue or cost flows among SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings. The filing states only at a general level that all such historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation as part of the common-control accounting. The specific dollar amounts disclosed in the related party transactions section pertain to dealings with **Tesla** and **The Boring Company**, which are related parties but remain **outside** the consolidated group and therefore are not subject to elimination. To identify the specific quantified intercompany flows among SpaceX, xAI, and X, one would likely need to consult the full consolidated financial statement notes (particularly any segment reporting or revenue disaggregation notes) that may provide more granular detail on intercompany activity prior to elimination.

results.content:
# Intercompany Transactions Between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings

## Key Finding: Eliminated but Not Detailed

After carefully reviewing all provided chunks, the filing **explicitly states** that intercompany transactions existed but were eliminated upon consolidation, without providing specific dollar amounts or descriptions of what those transactions were.

The critical disclosure appears in the merger accounting section:

> *"all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."*

This confirms that transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X **did occur** historically, but the filing does not itemize them.

---

## What Is NOT Found in the Provided Text

Despite searching for the specific categories you mentioned, the provided chunks contain **no specific disclosure** of:

| Category | Found? |
|---|---|
| Starlink services provided to xAI or X | ❌ Not detailed |
| AI services provided by xAI to SpaceX or X | ❌ Not detailed |
| Advertising on X by SpaceX or xAI | ❌ Not detailed (only Tesla's ad purchases on X are disclosed) |
| Data center services between entities | ❌ Not detailed |
| Cloud computing services between entities | ❌ Not detailed |
| Infrastructure sharing between entities | ❌ Not detailed |
| Employee secondments | ❌ Not detailed |
| Intersegment revenue or eliminations | ❌ Not shown in segment tables |

---

## What IS Disclosed: Related Party Transactions with External Affiliates

The filing instead details transactions with **external affiliated entities** (not between SpaceX/xAI/X themselves):

### Transactions with Tesla
- **SpaceX** obtained goods/services from Tesla: $11M (2023), $4M (2024), **$144M (2025)**, $0.2M (2026 YTD)
- **xAI** obtained goods/services from Tesla: $191M (2024), **$506M (2025)**, $34M (2026 YTD)
- **xAI** recognized revenue from Tesla: $2M (2025), $0.4M (2026 YTD)
- **X** received advertising purchases from Tesla: $0.5M (2024), $4M (2025), $0 (2026 YTD)
- **SpaceX** charged Tesla for aircraft usage: $1M (2023), $1M (2024), $2M (2025), $0 (2026 YTD)

### Transactions with Other Musk Entities
- **X** paid The Boring Company for office lease: $0.1M (2024), $1M (2025), $0.1M (2026 YTD)
- **SpaceX** paid The Boring Company for tunnel construction in Bastrop, TX: $1M (2025)
- **xAI** paid Musk Industries LLC for real property lease: $0.5M (2024), $2M (2025), $0.2M (2026 YTD)
- **SpaceX** paid Musk-owned security company: $2M (2023), $3M (2024), $4M (2025), $1M (2026 YTD)

### Transactions with Valor Equity Partners (Board Member Antonio Gracias)
- **xAI subsidiaries** entered into equipment lease, sublease, and access agreements with Valor (no dollar amounts disclosed in the provided text)

---

## Segment Reporting: No Intersegment Revenue Shown

The segment data provided includes:

| Metric | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 | FY 2025 | FY 2024 | FY 2023 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Connectivity** Segment Income from Operations | $1,188M | $1,033M | $4,423M | $2,006M | $469M |
| **AI** Segment Loss from Operations | $(2,469)M | $(936)M | $(6,355)M | $(1,561)M | $(3,973)M |

The segment tables do **not** include a line item for intersegment revenue or intersegment eliminations, suggesting either:
1. Intersegment transactions are not material enough to separately disclose, or
2. They are netted within consolidated totals without separate line-item presentation

---

## Strategic Collaboration Context (Not Intercompany per se)

The prospectus preamble describes **future** collaborative initiatives between the combined company and Tesla (an external affiliate), not between SpaceX/xAI/X:

- **Macrohard**: An AI project expected to run on both state-of-the-art processors and Tesla's next-generation processors
- **Terafab**: An AI chip manufacturing initiative to vertically integrate chip design, fabrication, and deployment
- Tesla's January 2026 investment in xAI, converted to SpaceX equity upon the xAI Merger

---

## Conclusion

The filing **acknowledges but does not itemize** intercompany transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings. The statement that "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation" is the only direct reference. All specifically quantified related party transactions in the provided text are with **external affiliated entities**—primarily Tesla, The Boring Company, Musk Industries LLC, a Musk-owned security company, and Valor Equity Partners. The absence of detailed intercompany transaction disclosure may reflect that these were eliminated in consolidation and therefore not required to be separately itemized, or that such details appear in portions of the S-1 not included in the provided chunks.

Based on the provided text chunks from the SpaceX S-1 filing, here is the information regarding segment reporting, the treatment of pre-merger transactions, and common control accounting:

**1. Segment Reporting and Intersegment Revenue**
The filing introduces three reportable segments—Space, Connectivity, and AI—but does not provide specific disclosures about intersegment revenue, intersegment eliminations, or detailed revenue reporting by segment in the provided text. The segments are defined as follows:
*   **Space segment:** Designs, manufactures, and launches reusable rockets to provide high cadence, reliable, and affordable access to space.
*   **Connectivity segment:** Operates a worldwide high-speed, low-latency broadband network powered by thousands of Starlink satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, delivering connectivity to consumer, enterprise, and government customers.
*   **AI segment:** Operates a vertically integrated AI platform spanning the frontier LLM Grok, AI solutions for consumer and enterprise customers, X (a real-time information, entertainment, and free speech platform), and AI computational infrastructure. 

**2. Treatment of Pre-Merger Transactions Between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings**
The financial statements have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented to include the historical results of xAI (acquired February 2, 2026) and X Holdings (acquired by xAI on March 28, 2025). Because these transactions were between entities under common control, the financial statements reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. 

**3. Common Control Accounting and Eliminations**
The filing explicitly details the common control accounting and what is eliminated:
*   **Common Control Justification:** "The Mergers have been accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control as Mr. Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in the Company, xAI and X through his majority voting interest in each such entity during the periods presented in these consolidated financial statements."
*   **What gets eliminated:** "No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and **all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation.**"
*   **Note 2 Policies:** Under the Summary of Significant Accounting Policies, it reiterates: "The consolidated financial statements include the accounts of the Company and its wholly owned subsidiaries. **All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation.**"
*   **Change in Reporting Entity:** The continuation of historical SpaceX equity combined with X and xAI equity (adjusted for exchange ratios) constitutes a change in reporting entity. Because the consolidated financial statements already reflect this reorganization for all periods presented, separate financial statements of xAI and X are not provided.

results.content:
Based on the provided text from the SpaceX S-1 filing, here is the detailed breakdown of the related party transactions note, specifically addressing the seven items you requested regarding services provided between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings prior to the mergers:

**1. Starlink connectivity services provided to xAI or X**
**Not mentioned.** The text details SpaceX's operation of Starlink and its global broadband network, but it does not explicitly disclose any related-party transaction where Starlink connectivity services were provided to xAI or X prior to the mergers. 

**2. AI services or compute provided by xAI to SpaceX or X**
**Not mentioned.** The text notes that xAI recognized revenue of $2 million in 2025 and $0.4 million from January 1, 2026 through February 28, 2026, but it specifically attributes this revenue to "commercial, licensing, and support agreements with Tesla." There is no mention of xAI providing AI services or compute to SpaceX or X.

**3. Advertising on X platform by SpaceX or xAI**
**Not mentioned.** The filing states that "Tesla has directly and indirectly purchased advertising on our X platform" (totaling $0.5 million in 2024, $4 million in 2025, and $0 in early 2026). However, there is no disclosure of SpaceX or xAI purchasing advertising on the X platform.

**4. Data center or colocation services**
**Not mentioned between SpaceX, xAI, and X.** The text extensively discusses xAI's massive computing equipment leases with Valor Equity Partners (three separate lease agreements with aggregate cash payments of $6,986 million, $6,633 million, and $6,587 million). It also mentions SpaceX's strategic vision for orbital data centers. However, there is no disclosure of data center or colocation services provided between SpaceX, xAI, and X themselves.

**5. Shared infrastructure**
**Not mentioned.** While the filing notes that the Mergers unite SpaceX’s launch capabilities and global connectivity network with xAI’s AI development capabilities, it does not disclose any specific historical shared infrastructure arrangements between SpaceX, xAI, and X prior to the mergers. 

**6. Cost sharing arrangements**
**Not mentioned.** There are no specific cost-sharing arrangements disclosed between SpaceX, xAI, and X. The only cost-sharing-esque arrangement detailed is for aircraft usage, where SpaceX owns and operates aircraft used by Elon Musk and Tesla personnel, and invoices Tesla for the use of such aircraft at rates determined by Tesla and SpaceX.

**7. Intercompany loans or financing arrangements**
**Not explicitly mentioned as loans.** However, there are significant financing-related transactions and notes:
*   **Equity Consideration:** Pursuant to the xAI Merger on February 2, 2026, SpaceX issued 321.7 million shares of Class A Common Stock, 121.7 million shares of Class B Common Stock, and paid $2,947 million in cash to holders of xAI Common Stock and Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock.
*   **Tesla's Investment in xAI:** In January 2026, Tesla agreed to invest $2,000 million in xAI via Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock. Following the xAI Merger, this right was converted into the right to acquire SpaceX Class A common stock, resulting in SpaceX issuing 3.8 million shares to Tesla on March 12, 2026.
*   **Lease Guarantees:** SpaceX (Space Exploration Technologies Corp.) guaranteed the lessees' payments and performance obligations for xAI's massive computing equipment leases with Valor.

**General Note on Inter-Entity Transactions:**
The filing explicitly states: *"The Mergers have been accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control... and all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."* This confirms that intercompany transactions did exist between SpaceX, xAI, and X, but the specific details of those internal services (like Starlink to xAI, etc.) are not broken out in the Related Party Transactions disclosure. Instead, the detailed financial amounts provided in the notes are strictly limited to transactions with *external* affiliated entities, such as Tesla, The Boring Company, Musk Industries LLC, a security company owned by Mr. Musk, and Valor Equity Partners.

Based on the provided S-1 filing chunks, here are the disclosures regarding the mergers, related party transactions, and segment interactions:

### 1. The xAI and X Holdings Mergers
*   **Timeline:** On March 28, 2025, xAI completed its acquisition of X Holdings Corp. (“X”) and X.AI Corp. (the "X Merger"). Later, on February 2, 2026 (the “xAI Merger Date”), SpaceX completed its acquisition of X.AI Holdings Corp. (“xAI”), making xAI a wholly-owned subsidiary of SpaceX.
*   **Accounting Treatment:** The mergers were accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control, as Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in SpaceX, xAI, and X. The financial statements reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets at historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. 
*   **Equity Adjustments:** The capital stock and shareholders’ equity reflect a continuation of historical SpaceX equity combined with X and xAI equity, adjusted by the exchange ratios used to effect the mergers. All of xAI’s redeemable convertible preferred stock was converted to SpaceX common stock as part of the merger. This presentation constitutes a change in reporting entity.

### 2. Elimination of Pre-Merger Transactions
The filing explicitly states: *"No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded and all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."*
*   **Specific Examples:** The provided text **does not** give specific examples or line-item details of what those pre-merger intercompany transactions were (e.g., it does not say "xAI paid SpaceX $X for launch services"). It only provides the general accounting policy that any such historical transactions between the entities were eliminated.

### 3. Related Party Transactions Note
The "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions" section primarily details transactions with *external* affiliated entities rather than internal segment-to-segment commercial agreements. Key related party disclosures include:
*   **Tesla:** SpaceX, xAI, and X have various commercial, licensing, support, and advertising agreements with Tesla. For example, xAI obtained $506 million in goods and services from Tesla in 2025. SpaceX also invoices Tesla for aircraft usage by Elon Musk and other Tesla personnel.
*   **The Boring Company:** X entered into an office lease with a Boring Company subsidiary, and SpaceX incurred $1 million in expenses in 2025 for tunnel construction by The Boring Company in Bastrop, Texas.
*   **Musk Industries LLC:** xAI leases real property from this entity, owned by Mr. Musk.
*   **Valor Equity Partners (Board member Antonio J. Gracias):** xAI subsidiaries have entered into massive computing equipment leases with Valor, providing for aggregate cash payments of approximately $20.2 billion ($6,986M + $6,633M + $6,587M) over the life of the leases. SpaceX guarantees these obligations. 
*   **Tesla's xAI Investment:** In January 2026, Tesla agreed to invest $2 billion in xAI's Series E Preferred Stock. Following the xAI Merger, this right converted into SpaceX Class A common stock, and SpaceX issued 3.8 million shares to Tesla in March 2026.

### 4. Segment Interactions and Synergies
The filing discusses how the segments interact strategically, though it frames these largely as competitive advantages and future strategic initiatives rather than formal commercial service agreements:
*   **AI (Grok) and X Integration:** The AI segment's Grok model is deeply integrated with the X platform. The filing states this gives Grok "freshness, relevance, and contextual awareness" and "direct, real-time access to the information and human discourse on X," which enhances Grok's truth-seeking capabilities.
*   **AI and Connectivity (Starlink):** The filing envisions orbital AI data centers. It states that SpaceX’s reusable rockets and satellite manufacturing can enable the deployment of "massive AI compute satellite constellations" in Sun-synchronous orbit to handle energy-intensive AI workloads. These orbital data centers would rely on **Starlink providing low-latency, global connectivity** to link them back to Earth.
*   **Space and AI:** SpaceX's launch capabilities and operational expertise are highlighted as the infrastructure that will allow xAI to rapidly construct AI compute infrastructure "starting on Earth with the goal of extending to space."

### 5. Commercial Agreements Between SpaceX, xAI, and X
The provided text **does not** disclose specific, ongoing commercial agreements (e.g., service-level agreements, transfer pricing, or intercompany billing) between the SpaceX, xAI, and X segments post-merger. The interactions described above are treated as strategic integrations and synergies of a unified company rather than arm's-length commercial contracts. The only specific commercial agreements detailed in the related party notes are with external entities like Tesla, The Boring Company, and Valor Equity Partners.

results.content:
# Analysis of Related Party Transactions Note

## Important Finding

Based on the chunks provided, **the filing does not enumerate specific pre-merger intercompany transactions between SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings that were eliminated in consolidation.** The disclosure is limited to a general statement:

> "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation"

The detailed "Related Party Transactions" note that follows focuses almost exclusively on transactions with **external** affiliated parties — primarily Tesla, The Boring Company, Musk-owned entities, and Valor Equity Partners — not on intercompany dealings among SpaceX, xAI, and X themselves.

## Responses to Your Five Specific Questions

### (1) xAI providing AI services or compute to X Holdings pre-X Merger
**Not found.** While the prospectus discusses Grok's integration with X (approximately 117 million MAUs using Grok's AI features as of March 31, 2026), this is described as a post-merger strategic benefit, not as a disclosed pre-merger related party transaction with quantified amounts.

### (2) X Holdings providing data or platform services to xAI pre-X Merger
**Not found.** The filing describes X's real-time data as a competitive differentiator for Grok ("Grok's deep integration with X enables freshness, relevance, and contextual awareness"), but this is presented as strategic narrative, not as a disclosed related party transaction with associated financial amounts that would have been eliminated.

### (3) SpaceX providing launch services or Starlink services to xAI or X Holdings
**Not found.** None of the provided chunks mention SpaceX supplying launch, Starlink broadband, or Starlink Mobile services to xAI or X as intercompany transactions. The SpaceX-Tesla commercial agreements are detailed (SpaceX obtained goods/services of $11M in 2023, $4M in 2024, $144M in 2025), but no equivalent disclosure exists for SpaceX-xAI or SpaceX-X service arrangements.

### (4) Shared employees or secondments between entities
**Not found.** The filing notes that Elon Musk "was a stockholder, director, and officer of each of xAI and X prior to the X Merger and the xAI Merger," but no disclosure of shared employees, secondments, or intercompany service arrangements between SpaceX, xAI, and X appears in the provided chunks. The security services disclosure involves a separate Musk-owned security company, not intercompany resource sharing.

### (5) Intercompany loans or financing
**Not found** in the context of SpaceX-xAI or SpaceX-X lending. The financing-related disclosures present are:
- **Tesla's xAI investment**: In January 2026, Tesla agreed to invest $2,000 million in xAI Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, which converted post-merger into 3.8 million SpaceX Class A shares (pre-split) issued March 12, 2026.
- **Valor Transaction II**: Recorded as a failed sale-leaseback with $5,365 million in debt — but this is with Valor (an external party), not an intercompany loan.
- **xAI Merger consideration**: 321.7 million Class A shares, 121.7 million Class B shares, and $2,947 million cash paid to xAI stockholders — this is merger consideration, not a loan.

---

## Full Text of What IS Disclosed in the Related Party Transactions Note

Here is the complete content from the provided chunks, organized by section:

### Scope and Thresholds

The note covers transactions since January 1, 2023 where:
- SpaceX was or is to be a participant
- The amount exceeded or will exceed $120,000
- Any director, executive officer, 5%+ stockholder, or their immediate family member/household member had or will have a direct or indirect material interest

The note explicitly states that post-xAI Merger, it includes "certain agreements and transactions originally entered into by xAI or X Holdings prior to the xAI Merger to the extent that such agreements and transactions are ongoing following the consummation of" the merger.

### Transactions with Elon Musk and Affiliated Entities

**Mr. Musk's affiliations**: Founder, CEO, CTO, Chairman of SpaceX; "Technoking," CEO, and director of Tesla (~20% shareholder as of November 10, 2025); founder of The Boring Company; was stockholder, director, and officer of xAI and X prior to the mergers.

**Tesla's SpaceX ownership**: Beneficial owner of 18,990,195 Class A shares as of May 1, 2026 — less than 1.0% of total outstanding after the offering.

**SpaceX–Tesla commercial, licensing and support agreements** (goods/services obtained by SpaceX):

| 2024 | $4 million |
| 2025 | $144 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $0.2 million |

|---|---|---|
| 2024 | $191 million | — |
| 2025 | $506 million | $2 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $34 million | $0.4 million |

|---|---|

| 2025 | $4 million |

**Aircraft usage** (SpaceX charging Tesla for aircraft use by Musk and Tesla personnel):
| Period | Amount Charged |
|---|---|
| 2023 | $1 million |
| 2024 | $1 million |
| 2025 | $2 million |

**X–Boring Company office lease** (X leasing from a Boring Company subsidiary):
| Period | Lease Payments |

| 2025 | $1 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $0.1 million |

**SpaceX–Boring Company tunnel construction**: SpaceX incurred $1 million in 2025 for tunnel construction by The Boring Company in Bastrop, Texas.

**xAI–Musk Industries LLC real property lease**:
| Period | Lease Payments |
|---|---|
| 2024 | $0.5 million |
| 2025 | $2 million |

|---|---|

### Transactions with Antonio J. Gracias and Valor Equity Partners

Mr. Gracias is a board member and founder/CEO/CIO of Valor Equity Partners.

**xAI–Valor equipment leases** (three separate computing equipment lease agreements):

| Lease | Aggregate Cash Payments Over Lease Life |
|---|---|
| Lease 1 | $6,986 million |
| Lease 2 | $6,633 million |
| Lease 3 | $6,587 million |
| **Total** | **$20,206 million** |

Lessee payments and performance obligations are guaranteed by Space Exploration Technologies Corp. or one of its subsidiaries.

Payments made under these leases:

|---|---|
| 2025 | $885 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $857 million |

**Valor Transaction II**: Deemed a failed sale-leaseback transaction; the Company recorded related debt of $5,365 million.

**X–Valor API services** (X received payments from Valor):
| Period | Amount |
|---|---|
| 2024 | $1 million |
| 2025 | $1 million |
| Jan 1–Feb 28, 2026 | $0.1 million |

### Other Transactions with Directors and Executive Officers

SpaceX owns and operates, through subsidiary Falcon Landing, LLC, three aircraft for use by directors, executive officers, and others. (The chunk cuts off here in the provided text.)

### xAI Merger Consideration

On February 2, 2026, SpaceX issued (pre-2026 Stock Split):
- 321.7 million shares of Class A Common Stock
- 121.7 million shares of Class B Common Stock
- $2,947 million in cash

...to holders of xAI Common Stock and Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock.

### Tesla's xAI Investment and SpaceX Class A Issuance

- January 2026: Tesla agreed to invest $2,000 million in xAI Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock
- Conditioned on regulatory approvals
- Post-xAI Merger: Tesla's right converted into right to acquire SpaceX Class A common stock
- March 12, 2026: SpaceX issued 3.8 million Class A shares (pre-split basis) to Tesla

---

## Key Takeaway

The filing's approach to the eliminated intercompany transactions is notably **non-specific**. While it states that "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation," it does **not** enumerate what those transactions were, their nature, or their dollar amounts. This is consistent with standard accounting practice for common-control combinations — ASC 805-50 requires elimination of intercompany balances and transactions but does not require separate disclosure of each eliminated item. The detailed related party disclosures that follow focus on transactions with **external** related parties (Tesla, Boring Company, Valor, Musk-owned entities) that survive consolidation because they involve parties outside the SpaceX-xAI-X corporate family.

The absence of specific intercompany transaction disclosures means that based on the provided chunks, **none of your five categories of pre-merger intercompany transactions are specifically identified** in the filing.

Based on the provided text from the SpaceX S-1 filing, here is the disclosure information regarding the mergers and the pre-merger commercial relationships between the entities:

**1. Disclosure about the X Merger (March 28, 2025) and the xAI Merger (February 2, 2026)**
*   **Transaction Mechanics:** Both the X Merger and the xAI Merger were effected through a share exchange. On March 28, 2025, xAI completed its acquisition of X Holdings Corp. (“X”) and X.AI Corp., making them wholly-owned subsidiaries of xAI. Subsequently, on February 2, 2026, SpaceX completed its acquisition of X.AI Holdings Corp. (“xAI”), making xAI a wholly-owned subsidiary of SpaceX.
*   **Accounting Treatment:** The Mergers were accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control, as Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in SpaceX, xAI, and X through his majority voting interest. The consolidated financial statements were retrospectively recast to combine the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. No new goodwill or intangible assets were recorded.
*   **Elimination of Intercompany Transactions:** The filing states that "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation." 

**2. Disclosure about commercial agreements between xAI and X Holdings before the X Merger**
The provided text **does not explicitly disclose** the existence of specific pre-merger commercial agreements between xAI and X Holdings, such as xAI licensing Grok to X, X providing data to xAI for training, or revenue-sharing arrangements. 
*   The only direct reference to their historical financial relationship is the blanket statement that "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."
*   However, the filing heavily emphasizes their current strategic integration post-merger, noting that X serves as a "foundational distribution and data engine for our AI ecosystem." It highlights that Grok's "deep integration with X enables freshness, relevance, and contextual awareness" and that "direct, real-time access to the information and human discourse on X enhances Grok’s truth-seeking capabilities by grounding outputs in up-to-date knowledge."

**3. Disclosure about SpaceX's commercial relationships with xAI or X Holdings before the mergers**
The provided text **does not disclose** any specific pre-merger commercial agreements between SpaceX and xAI or X Holdings. 
*   As with the xAI/X relationship, it notes that historical related party transactions between SpaceX and the other entities were eliminated in the consolidation.
*   The filing instead focuses on the post-merger strategic synergy, noting that the acquisition "unites SpaceX’s launch capabilities and global connectivity network with xAI’s AI development capabilities." It outlines future collaborative visions, such as using SpaceX's reusable rockets and satellite manufacturing to deploy massive AI compute satellite constellations for orbital data centers.

results.content:
Based on the provided S-1 filing text, here is the complete revenue recognition policy and related disclosures concerning the Space, Connectivity, and AI segments, specifically addressing your four points of inquiry:

**(1) How Starlink satellite launches are treated (intersegment revenue recognition)**
The filing explicitly states that no intersegment revenue is recognized for Starlink launches. Instead, the costs are capitalized within the Connectivity segment. The exact language is:
> "For launches of our Starlink satellites, the Company does not recognize any inter-segment revenue, rather those launch costs are capitalized in satellites in Property, plant, and equipment, net."
> 
> "For launches dedicated to deploying our Starlink satellites, we capitalize the associated costs within our Connectivity segment and depreciate them over time, and we do not recognize revenue for those launches in our Space segment."

**(2) How launch capacity allocated to the Connectivity segment or AI segment is treated**
The filing notes that a significant amount of launch capacity is allocated to Connectivity, with future allocations planned for AI, and that the Space segment's reported revenue strictly excludes this internal capacity usage. The exact language is:
> "We allocate a significant amount of launch capacity to our Connectivity segment, and expect to allocate a significant amount to our AI segment in the future. Our Space segment revenue only reflects our customer launches and customer activities."
> 
> "As a result, notwithstanding an increasing launch cadence, our Space segment has relatively lower revenue scale and revenue growth compared to our other segments, though its financial results do not reflect the foundational strategic value that it provides to us in bolstering the growth of our Connectivity and AI segments."

**(3) Disclosure about intersegment pricing or transfer pricing**
The provided text **does not contain** specific disclosures regarding intersegment pricing or transfer pricing methodologies. The only disclosure regarding intersegment financial interactions is the explicit statement that no inter-segment revenue is recognized for internal Starlink launches, as quoted in point (1): *"the Company does not recognize any inter-segment revenue..."*

**(4) Disclosure about how the segments interact commercially**
The filing describes the segments as a "vertically integrated innovation engine" where the Space segment provides foundational infrastructure (launch capacity and satellites) that enables the Connectivity and AI segments to operate and grow. The exact language detailing these commercial interactions includes:
> "We have three reportable segments in our vertically integrated innovation engine: Space, Connectivity, and AI. In our Space segment, we design, manufacture, and launch reusable rockets to provide high cadence, reliable, and affordable access to space at unprecedented scale. In our Connectivity segment, we operate a worldwide high-speed, low-latency broadband data and communications network powered by over 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, delivering connectivity to millions of consumer, enterprise, and government customers across 164 countries, territories, and other markets. In our AI segment, we operate a highly vertically integrated AI platform spanning our truth-seeking frontier model Grok, AI solutions for consumer and enterprise customers, X—our real-time information, entertainment, and free speech platform—and AI computational infrastructure."

Additionally, the filing details a future commercial interaction where the Connectivity segment (via Starlink) will support the AI segment's orbital compute infrastructure:
> "...satellites in Sun-synchronous orbit will be able to handle energy-intensive AI workloads, such as inference demand, at far greater scale and efficiency than terrestrial alternatives, with Starlink providing low-latency, global connectivity linking these orbital AI systems to people around the world and delivering real-time intelligence. We expect to begin deploying our orbital AI compute satellites as early as 2028."

And, as noted in point (2), the Space segment is described as providing foundational strategic value to the other two segments:
> "...its financial results do not reflect the foundational strategic value that it provides to us in bolstering the growth of our Connectivity and AI segments."

Based on the provided S-1 filing chunks, here are the disclosures regarding the AI segment's revenue, X's revenue streams, related party revenue, and the revenue disaggregation note:

### 1. AI Segment Revenue and X Platform Integration
The AI segment generates revenue from the sale of digital platform services, which includes advertising, subscription, and licensing services offered to consumers and enterprise customers. Specifically, the AI segment generates revenue from:
*   **Advertising:** The sale of ad products displayed on the X platform. Revenue is recognized in the period when advertising is delivered (e.g., user engagement). 
*   **AI Solutions and Infrastructure:** This includes:
    *   **Premium subscriptions** on X and Grok (recognized ratably over the subscription term, ranging from month-to-month to one year).
    *   **Data licensing arrangements** (recognized ratably over the period, from month-to-month to two years).
    *   **API access** to Grok models (recognized ratably over the contract term for stand-ready access or as consumed for usage-based arrangements).

The filing notes that in 2023 and 2024, substantially all of the AI segment's revenue consisted of advertising, subscriptions, and data licensing revenue generated from X (formerly known as Twitter). By 2025 and 2026, Grok subscriptions and API access became significant growth drivers. For example, in the year ended December 31, 2025, AI solutions and infrastructure revenue increased by $465 million, mainly due to an increase in X and Grok subscription revenue ($365 million) and data licensing arrangements ($88 million). 

### 2. Intercompany Revenue from X to xAI / Eliminated Related Party Revenue
Regarding intercompany revenue between X, xAI, and SpaceX:
*   **Elimination of Related Party Transactions:** The filing states that X and xAI merged into SpaceX under common control. As a result of these mergers, "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation." Therefore, any historical intercompany revenue (such as from X to xAI) is eliminated and not recognized as external revenue in the consolidated financial statements.
*   **Related Party Revenue from External Parties:** The only disclosed related-party revenue from external entities involving X or xAI comes from **Tesla**:
    *   **xAI commercial, licensing, and support agreements:** xAI recognized revenue of $2 million in 2025 and $0.4 million from January 1, 2026 through February 28, 2026 from Tesla.
    *   **X Holdings advertising agreements:** Tesla purchased advertising on the X platform totaling $0.5 million in 2024, $4 million in 2025, and $0 from January 1, 2026 through February 28, 2026.

### 3. Revenue Disaggregation Note
Revenue is disaggregated by type and segment across three segments: Space, Connectivity, and AI. 

**For the Year Ended December 31, 2025:**
*   **Space Segment:** $4,086 million (Launch Services: $2,576 million; Launch & Development: $1,510 million)
*   **Connectivity Segment:** $11,387 million (Consumer: $7,208 million; Enterprise & Government: $4,179 million)

results.content:
# Analysis of Requested Disclosures

After carefully reviewing all provided chunks, here are my findings for each of the five items:

---

## (1) X Merger Consideration and Pre-Merger Commercial Agreements Between xAI and X

**X Merger consideration — found.** The text discloses the exchange ratio:

> "Upon the effective date of the X Merger, each class of common stock of X Holdings Corp. ("X Common Stock") was converted to 2.776 shares of xAI Common Stock of the same class (rounded down to the nearest whole share)"

The all-stock nature is confirmed by:

> "The Mergers were each effected through a share exchange."

**Pre-existing commercial agreements between xAI and X before March 28, 2025 — not found.** The text does not describe any specific commercial agreements that existed between xAI and X prior to the X Merger date. The only general statement is:

> "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation"

This confirms such transactions existed but does not itemize what they were.

---

## (2) Grok Licensed to X or X Providing Data to xAI Before the Merger

**Not specifically found.** The text describes the current (post-merger) integration as a competitive advantage:

> "Grok's deep integration with X enables freshness, relevance, and contextual awareness that we believe is a competitive differentiator. This direct, real-time access to the information and human discourse on X enhances Grok's truth-seeking capabilities by grounding outputs in up-to-date knowledge and diverse viewpoints."

And:

> "approximately 117 million MAUs that used Grok's AI features as of March 31, 2026"

However, there is **no explicit disclosure** stating that Grok was licensed to X under a commercial agreement, or that X provided data to xAI under a commercial data arrangement, prior to the merger. The text does not distinguish between pre-merger and post-merger data access arrangements.

---

## (3) All-Stock Nature of the X Merger and Prior Commercial Relationships

**All-stock nature — confirmed.** As quoted above:

> "The Mergers were each effected through a share exchange."

And the specific X Merger ratio:

> "each class of common stock of X Holdings Corp. ("X Common Stock") was converted to 2.776 shares of xAI Common Stock of the same class"

**Suggestion of prior commercial relationships — not explicitly found.** The text does not frame the X Merger as formalizing or consolidating pre-existing commercial arrangements between xAI and X. The rationale given for the mergers is framed around common control and strategic integration, not prior commercial ties:

> "The Mergers have been accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control as Mr. Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in the Company, xAI and X through his majority voting interest in each such entity during the periods presented in these consolidated financial statements."

The only indirect acknowledgment that inter-entity transactions existed is the elimination language:

> "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation"

---

## (4) X's Advertising, Subscription, or Data Licensing Revenue Recognition Before vs. After the Merger

**Current revenue recognition policies — found, but no before/after comparison.** The text describes the company's current policies:

> "The Company generates revenue from (i) the sale of ad products displayed on its X platform, and (ii) providing AI solutions and infrastructure, which includes subscription-related offerings, data licensing arrangements, and API"

For advertising:

> "Revenue for advertising services is recognized in the period when advertising is delivered as evidenced by a person engaging with an ad on the Company's platforms in a manner satisfying the types of engagement selected by the advertisers. The Company's contract terms for advertising services are typically cancellable short-term arrangements."

For subscriptions and data licensing:

> "Revenue for AI solutions and infrastructure includes: (i) premium subscriptions on X and Grok which is recognized ratably over the period of the subscription term (ranging from month-to-month to one year), (ii) data licensing revenue which is generally recognized ratably over the period (from month-to-month to two years) in which the"

**No disclosure was found** comparing how X's revenue recognition differed before versus after the merger. The text presents these as the company's current policies without distinguishing pre-merger X practices.

---

## (5) Intercompany Services Between SpaceX and xAI or X

**General elimination statement — found; specific services — not found.** The only disclosure is the blanket statement:

> "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation"

The Related Party Transactions section focuses on transactions with **Tesla** and **The Boring Company** — entities outside the SpaceX/xAI/X consolidated group. Specific examples found include:

- **SpaceX–Tesla commercial agreements:** "we obtained goods and services of $11 million in 2023, $4 million in 2024, $144 million in 2025, and $0.2 million from January 1, 2026 through February 28"
- **xAI–Tesla commercial agreements:** "xAI obtained goods and services of $191 million in 2024, $506 million in 2025, and $34 million from January 1, 2026 through February 28, 2026"
- **X–Tesla advertising:** "Tesla has directly and indirectly purchased advertising on our X platform. These amounts totaled $0.5 million in 2024, $4 million in 2025, and $0 from January 1, 2026 through"
- **Aircraft usage with Tesla:** "we charged Tesla $1 million in 2023, $1 million in 2024, $2 million in 2025, and $0 from January 1, 2026 through February 28"
- **X–Boring Company lease:** "In 2024, X entered into a lease for office space with a subsidiary owned by The Boring Company"

**No specific disclosure** was found itemizing intercompany services rendered between SpaceX and xAI, or between SpaceX and X, either before or after the mergers. The text confirms such transactions existed (via the elimination language) but does not describe their nature or amounts.

---

## Summary

| Item | Found? |
|------|--------|
| (1) X Merger consideration | **Yes** — 2.776 xAI shares per X share, via share exchange |
| (1) Pre-merger xAI–X commercial agreements | **No** — only generic elimination language |
| (2) Grok licensed to X / X data to xAI pre-merger | **No** — only post-merger integration described |
| (3) All-stock nature confirmed | **Yes** — "share exchange" |
| (3) Suggestion of prior commercial relationships | **No** — framed as common-control reorganization |
| (4) Revenue recognition before vs. after merger | **No** — current policies described, no comparison |
| (5) Intercompany services SpaceX↔xAI/X | **No** — only generic elimination; specifics limited to Tesla/Boring Company |

# Common Control and Merger Accounting Notes — Complete Text from the Provided Chunks

Below is every relevant passage I can locate in the chunks you provided, organized by your four questions plus the commercial-agreements question.

---

## 1. How the Mergers Were Accounted For

**From the merger timeline / Note 1 chunk:**

> "On February 2, 2026 ('xAI Merger Date'), the Company completed its acquisition of X.AI Holdings Corp. ('xAI'), pursuant to which xAI became a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company ('xAI Merger'). Prior to the xAI Merger, on March 28, 2025, xAI completed its acquisition of X Holdings Corp. ('X') and X.AI Corp., in which X and X.AI Corp. became wholly-owned subsidiaries of xAI ('X Merger', and collectively with xAI Merger, 'Mergers'). X.AI Corp began operations in March 2023 and Twitter, Inc. ('Twitter') was acquired by Mr. Elon Musk in October 2022. The Mergers were each effected through a share exchange."

> "The Mergers have been accounted for as reorganizations of entities under common control as Mr. Elon Musk had a controlling financial interest in the Company, xAI and X through his majority voting interest in each such entity during the years presented in these consolidated financial statements. The Company's consolidated financial statements have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the net assets of the entities at their historical carrying amounts for all periods presented. No new goodwill or other intangible assets have been recorded…"

**From the prospectus preamble chunk:**

> "Our audited consolidated financial statements and related notes have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the companies for all periods presented as the acquisitions of xAI and X Holdings were accounted for as transactions between entities under common control."

**From the financial summary preamble chunk:**

> "The summary historical consolidated financial and operating data of SpaceX has been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the companies for all periods presented to include the historical results of xAI, which was acquired by SpaceX, effective February 2, 2026, and X Holdings, which was acquired by xAI, effective March 28, 2025, because these transactions were between entities under common control."

**From the retrospectively recast chunk:**

> "The consolidated financial statements of SpaceX have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented to include (i) the historical results of X.AI Holdings Corp., which was acquired by SpaceX, effective February 2, 2026 (the 'xAI Merger'), and X Holdings Corp. ('X Holdings'), which was acquired by xAI, effective March 28, 2025 (the 'X Merger'), because these transactions were between entities under common control, and (ii) a five-for-one stock split of the Company's Class A, Class B, and Class C Common Stock, effective May 4, 2026 (the '2026 Stock Split')."

**Equity continuation language:**

> "The capital stock and shareholders' equity for all periods presented reflects a continuation of the historical SpaceX capital stock and shareholders' equity, combined with the historical capital stock and shareholders' equity of X and xAI merged under common control, as adjusted by the respective exchange ratios used to effect the Mergers, except for xAI's historical redeemable convertible preferred stock through the date of the xAI Merger."

**Change in reporting entity:**

> "This presentation constitutes a change in reporting entity. Refer to Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity for additional details." *(One chunk references Note 12; another references Note 13 — the note number appears to differ between draft versions.)*

**Omission of separate financial statements:**

> "As the consolidated financial statements already reflect the reorganization of entities under common control for all periods presented, separate financial statements of xAI and X are not provided."

**2026 Stock Split (retroactive adjustment):**

> "On May 4, 2026, the Company effected a five-for-one forward stock split of its authorized, issued, and outstanding shares of Class A, Class B, and Class C Common Stock ('2026 Stock Split'). The conversion rate of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock was proportionately adjusted to factor in the 2026 Stock Split. All share and per share information has been retroactively adjusted to reflect the 2026 Stock Split for all periods presented."

**xAI Merger consideration:**

> "Pursuant to the terms of the xAI Merger on February 2, 2026, the Company issued, prior to the 2026 Stock Split, 321.7 million shares of Class A Common Stock, 121.7 million shares of Class B Common Stock and paid $2,947 million in cash to holders of xAI Common Stock and Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock."

**Exchange ratio detail:**

> "Upon the effective date of the xAI Merger, every outstanding share of xAI Common Stock, whether Class A, Class B, or Limited Voting, converted into the right to receive SpaceX Common Stock at a fixed exchange ratio of 0.1433 SpaceX shares per share of xAI Common Stock, unless the holder was an eligible service provider and elected to receive cash of $75.46 per share of xAI Class A or Class B (on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis). No fractional SpaceX shares were issued and all share amounts were rounded up to the nearest whole number. Any shares of xAI Common Stock previously held by the Company were canceled and retired and did not receive any consideration."

**xAI warrant treatment at merger:**

> "xAI also issued warrants to customers that were outstanding as of the effective date of the xAI Merger, which had a ten-year term originally set to expire in 2035, with an exercise price equal to the par value of the stock, and vesting terms that resulted in the warrants vesting proportionally to the payments received under the related agreement. The closing of the xAI Merger triggered an acceleration clause in which all outstanding xAI warrants, both vested and unvested components, were automatically exercised on a cashless basis exercised and converted into fully vested SpaceX Class A Common Stock at the exchange ratio of 0.1433 (on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis)."

---

## 2. What Was Eliminated

**Intercompany transactions and balances (general consolidation policy — Note 2):**

> "The consolidated financial statements include the accounts of the Company and its wholly owned subsidiaries. All intercompany balances and transactions have been eliminated in consolidation."

**Historical related party transactions between the merged entities:**

> "…and all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."

This sentence appears in both the first preamble chunk and the merger-timeline chunk, confirming that the elimination applies to transactions that were *between* SpaceX, xAI, and X during the historical periods now presented on a combined basis.

---

## 3. Specific Examples of Eliminated Transactions

**The provided chunks do not contain any specific, itemized examples of eliminated intercompany transactions** between SpaceX, xAI, and X. The filing states the elimination only in general terms ("all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation"). No dollar amounts, contract types, or line-item descriptions of the eliminated transactions are disclosed in the chunks provided.

---

## 4. Treatment of Historical Related Party Transactions

The filing draws a distinction between two categories:

### (a) Transactions *between* the merged entities (eliminated)

> "…all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation."

Because the entities are now combined retrospectively as if always under common control, any transactions they had with each other in prior periods are treated as intercompany and removed.

### (b) Transactions with *external* related parties (retained and disclosed)

The "Related Party Transactions" section continues to disclose SpaceX's relationships with entities that remain *outside* the consolidated group — most notably **Tesla** — because those transactions were not between the merged entities and therefore survive consolidation:

> "Mr. Musk … was a stockholder, director, and officer of each of xAI and X prior to the X Merger and the xAI Merger. We have certain relationships and/or transactions with Mr. Musk and affiliated entities, as described below."

> "Tesla is the beneficial owner of 18,990,195 shares of our Class A common stock as of May 1, 2026, representing an ownership interest of less than 1.0% of the total outstanding number of shares of our Class A common stock, after giving effect to the sale of shares of Class A common stock in this offering."

> "We have historically collaborated with Tesla through commercial, licensing, and support agreements. Certain amounts presented below that may have been incurred in one year could be paid in another year."

> "SpaceX commercial, licensing and support agreements. We are party with Tesla to certain agreements which generally relate to commercial, licensing, and support agreements and standardized commercial transactions with Tesla done on terms no less favorable to SpaceX than those generally available to unaffiliated third parties under similar circumstances. Pursuant to those agreements, we obtained goods and services of $11 million in…"

**Tesla's xAI investment (pre-merger related-party transaction that converted post-merger):**

> "In January 2026, Tesla entered into an agreement with xAI to invest $2,000 million via a purchase of xAI Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock. Pursuant to the terms of that agreement and a letter agreement entered into between xAI and Tesla on January 16, 2026, xAI's issuance of the shares of Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, and Tesla's payment therefore, was conditioned upon the receipt of required regulatory approvals. Following the xAI Merger, Tesla's right to acquire Series E Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock of xAI was converted into the right to acquire SpaceX Class A common stock. On March 12, 2026, following expiration of the applicable regulatory waiting period, SpaceX issued 3.8 million shares of Class A Common Stock (on a pre-2026 Stock Split basis) to Tesla in accordance with the terms of the foregoing agreements."

**Aircraft-related related-party transactions (retained disclosures):**

> "We own and operate, through our subsidiary, Falcon Landing, LLC, three aircraft for use by our directors, executive officers and employees in connection with the performance of their duties for business purposes. One of the aircraft is maintained and serviced by Craft Aviation Services, LLC, an affiliate of Mr. Musk. The amount of the expenses incurred by us for the maintenance and service of this aircraft was $1 million in 2023, $1 million in 2024, $3 million in 2025, and $1 million from January 1, 2026 through February 28, 2026."

> "In certain circumstances, when our aircraft are unavailable, Mr. Musk uses his personal aircraft for SpaceX business purposes and is reimbursed by us… SpaceX has incurred expenses of $0.1 million in 2023, $3 million in 2024, $2 million in 2025, and $0.2 million from January 1, 2026 through February 28, 2026."

---

## 5. Disclosure About Commercial Agreements Between the Entities Before the Mergers

**The provided chunks do not contain explicit disclosure of pre-merger commercial agreements *between* SpaceX and xAI, or between SpaceX and X, or between xAI and X.** The filing states only that:

- All such inter-entity related party transactions were "eliminated in consolidation."
- Mr. Musk "was a stockholder, director, and officer of each of xAI and X prior to the X Merger and the xAI Merger."

The only pre-merger commercial/strategic relationship *between an external party and one of the merged entities* that is specifically described is **Tesla's January 2026 agreement to invest $2 billion in xAI Series E preferred stock**, which post-merger converted into SpaceX Class A shares.

The chunks do describe SpaceX's ongoing commercial, licensing, and support agreements with **Tesla** (an entity that remains outside the consolidated group), but these are not inter-entity transactions subject to elimination — they are third-party related-party transactions that continue to be disclosed.

---

## Summary of What Is and Is Not in the Provided Chunks

| Question | Answered? | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| How mergers were accounted for | ✅ Yes | Common-control reorganizations; retrospective combination at historical carrying amounts; no goodwill; change in reporting entity |
| What was eliminated | ✅ Yes | "All historical related party transactions between the entities" and "all intercompany balances and transactions" |
| Specific examples of eliminated transactions | ❌ No | No itemized examples or dollar amounts provided in the chunks |
| Treatment of historical related party transactions | ✅ Partially | Inter-entity transactions eliminated; external related-party transactions (Tesla, Craft Aviation, Musk aircraft) retained and disclosed |
| Pre-merger commercial agreements between the entities | ❌ No | Not explicitly described; only stated that they were eliminated in consolidation |

Let me think about this question carefully. The question asks me to identify two specific pre-merger revenue or cost flows among SpaceX, xAI, and X Holdings that are eliminated in consolidation under common-control recast accounting, and for each, explain whether elimination inflates or deflates reported seller-segment economics.

Based on my research, the filing explicitly states that "all historical related party transactions between the entities have been eliminated in consolidation" but does NOT itemize specific dollar amounts or descriptions of what those transactions were. The detailed related party disclosures focus on transactions with external affiliated entities (Tesla, The Boring Company, Valor, etc.).

However, I did find one specific disclosure that is highly relevant:

1. **Starlink satellite launches by the Space segment for the Connectivity segment** - The filing explicitly states:
   > "For launches of our Starlink satellites, the Company does not recognize any inter-segment revenue, rather those launch costs are capitalized in satellites in Property, plant, and equipment, net."
   > "For launches dedicated to deploying our Starlink satellites, we capitalize the associated costs within our Connectivity segment and depreciate them over time, and we do not recognize revenue for those launches in our Space segment."
   > "We allocate a significant amount of launch capacity to our Connectivity segment, and expect to allocate a significant amount to our AI segment in the future. Our Space segment revenue only reflects our customer launches and customer activities."

This is a specific inter-segment flow that is eliminated (or rather, not recognized as revenue). The Space segment provides launch services to the Connectivity segment (and will to the AI segment), but no inter-segment revenue is recognized. This DEFLATES the Space segment's reported revenue/economics because the Space segment's revenue only reflects customer launches, not the significant launch capacity allocated to internal Starlink deployments.

The elimination DEFLATES the Space segment's reported revenue because the Space segment's revenue only reflects customer launches, not internal Starlink deployments.
